items.text: 1. Skeletons as data: a single source in the model maps each type (discovery / planning / execution / closeout) to its `## DoD` skeleton. (Evidence: the mapping in source.)

2. `open` seeds the right skeleton: `lume open --type <t>` creates the iteration with that type's DoD skeleton; the Self-review/Handback/Verdict scaffold is identical across types. (Evidence: open each of the four types -> its DoD section matches the skeleton; scaffold unchanged.)

3. Execution unchanged (common case): `execution` (the default) keeps today's generic `- [x] (propose checkable items)` so the default `lume open` body is byte-for-byte what it was pre-007. (Evidence: open execution -> identical to the prior template.)

4. Skeletons are prompts, not auto-DoDs: the seed is a starting prompt; the iteration still lands at `proposed` and the DoD must still be made crisp + operator-approved (gate unchanged). A defensive fallback to the execution skeleton covers any unmapped type. (Evidence: phase `proposed` after open; source fallback.)
5. Non-negotiables: no inference/network; no commit; `open` still writes only the new iteration file + the auto-refreshed snapshot; gates/transitions unchanged. (Evidence: scope; grep.)
6. Tests: open each of the four types asserts its DoD skeleton; execution matches the prior generic template; full suite green. (Evidence: count + run.)

handback:
What changed (uncommitted):
- `iteration.py`: added `_SKELETONS` (per-type `## DoD` seed prompts, data) and a `{dod}` placeholder in `_BODY_TEMPLATE`; `Iteration.new` seeds the skeleton for the iteration's type, falling back to the execution (generic) seed for unmapped types. The discovery/planning/closeout seeds mirror what those stages actually did this session (002/003) and build-lume's retro - grounded, not invented.
- Tests: 3 new cases in `test_iteration.py` (per-type seeds + constant scaffold; execution byte-identical to the old default; unmapped fallback). 77 total, green.
Per-type behaviour beyond the DoD seed (templates as data, the contract proof) remains P5.
self_review:
Adversarial pass against real evidence:
1. PASS - `_SKELETONS` in `iteration.py` is the single source mapping discovery/planning/execution/closeout to a `## DoD` seed. Data, keyed by type.
2. PASS - `Iteration.new` selects `_SKELETONS[type]` and fills the shared body template; the Self-review/Handback/Verdict scaffold is one template, identical across types. Live: `lume open --type discovery` seeded the discovery DoD (Context built / Artifact / Scope held); `test_new_seeds_per_type_dod_skeleton` checks discovery/planning/closeout markers + the constant scaffold.
3. PASS - execution keeps the original generic seed `- [ ] (propose checkable items)`; `test_execution_keeps_generic_skeleton` asserts it AND that the no-type default produces an identical body (so the common-case `lume open` body is unchanged from pre-007).
4. PASS - the seed is a prompt: `open` still lands at `proposed` (unchanged), the DoD is edited + approved through the normal gate; `Iteration.new` falls back to the execution skeleton for any unmapped type (`test_unmapped_type_falls_back_to_execution_skeleton`).
5. PASS - no inference/network (grep clean); no commit; `open` writes only the new iteration file + the auto-refreshed snapshot (path unchanged); gates/transitions untouched. git scope: iteration.py + test_iteration.py.
6. PASS - `python3 -m unittest discover`: Ran 77 tests, OK (3 new skeleton cases in test_iteration.py).

Diff swept: skeletons live only in the model; `_BODY_TEMPLATE` gained a single `{dod}` placeholder; no unused code.
